title: Calf reduction Botox: Is this a must-have treatment for your clinic？
filename: Calf reduction Botox： Is this a must-have treatment for your clinic？.en.vtt
excerpt: So interesting question about using toxin in the body [Music] so if you want to reduce the size of a muscle you can reduce the size of muscles in the shoulders and in the calf and this is particularly popular in patients from the far east and this is the question is should you be…

segments.text: so interesting question about using toxin in the body [Music] so if you want to reduce the size of a muscle you can reduce the size of muscles in the shoulders and in the calf and this is particularly popular in patients from the far east and this is the question is should you be offering this in your clinic well it depends on how many patients you have requesting about whether you decide that it's worth training up to be able to do it the next question you may ask yourself is can you perceive a psychosocial impact on this treatment now i know not a lot of practitioners ask this question but i believe from a healthcare practitioner perspective we should be improving the health of our patients with every procedure that we do otherwise you cannot ethically justify intervening with a risky procedure unless you think there is going to be a health benefit so that's the medical model and we are all actually obliged to behave that way i know many people don't but that's actually what we should be doing as healthcare professionals so you need to ask

yourself and ask your patient is this going to improve your life in some way and try and hone that down is this really a problem in terms of confidence or in terms of what they feel free to be able to do are they covering themselves up instead of wearing skirts for example and will this enable them to live a freer fuller life then you could learn to do the procedure and it will help don't i've certainly been in a case with one of those patients who wanted it who thought it was going to prevent her calves getting fat and had a couple of treatments and actually was okay with the way her calves were already it had been a couple of years so i knew what her natural state was and she was still under the impression that it was somehow preventative and i did not treat her um so that that's the kind of thing you want to be careful of you don't be doing treatments that benefit you but have no benefit to the patient and as long as you're happy that that's not the case then by all means pick up a new skill and offer it to those people who are going to benefit from it
